changelog.summary: Initial seven-phase verifiable-credential / digital-wallet verifier-trust playbook. Covers the trust-verification failure modes a credential verifier exposes when it accepts SD-JWT-VC, OID4VCI/OID4VP, mdoc (ISO 18013-5), DID-identified, status-list-governed, or OpenID-Federation-anchored credentials: issuer key not pinned to a trust anchor, revocation/status not enforced, did:web resolution unpinned, presentations accepted without nonce/audience key-binding (replay), mdoc device-auth skipped, open signature-algorithm sets, unverified key attestation, federation chains not anchored, over-disclosure beyond the DCQL query, and status-list issuers outside the credential issuer trust scope. Closes the GRC loop against NIST 800-53 IA-9 / IA-5, ISO 27001 A.5.16 / A.8.5, NIS2 Art.21 identity-of-entities, and the EU Digital Identity Wallet (eIDAS 2.0) high-assurance expectations. Cross-cuts identity-sso-compromise (federation trust) and cred-stores (wallet key material).

preconditions.description: Agent must be able to read the operator's credential-verifier source and/or its runtime configuration to inspect how issuer keys, revocation, DID resolution, presentation key-binding, and algorithm policy are trusted. A black-box host with neither source nor config marks the playbook visibility_gap=no_verifier_inventory.

theater_fingerprints.claim: We accept the EUDI / mDL wallet, so our acceptance is trustworthy.
theater_fingerprints.fast_detection_test: Wallet certification covers the WALLET; ask for the VERIFIER trust-anchor config. If the verifier accepts any self-consistent issuer key, certification is irrelevant.

theater_fingerprints.claim: The credential signature verifies, so the credential is trusted.
theater_fingerprints.fast_detection_test: A valid signature only proves the holder of SOME key signed it. Ask which trust anchor the issuer key was validated against; "it verified" without anchor-pinning is theater.

theater_fingerprints.claim: Our credentials support a status list, so revocation is handled.
theater_fingerprints.fast_detection_test: Grep the verifier accept-path for a status fetch + bit check. Supporting a status_list claim the verifier never reads is theater.

framework_context.gap_summary: Org frameworks treat "identity verified" as a binary and assume a validated signature equals a trusted issuer. None of NIST 800-53, ISO 27001, or NIS2 prescribe issuer-trust-anchor pinning, presentation replay-binding, or revocation enforcement for the verifiable-credential / wallet acceptance path — the controls predate the wallet ecosystem.

per_framework_gaps.insufficient_because: does not address verifiable-credential issuer trust-anchor pinning or presentation key-binding; written for service-to-service auth, not wallet-presented credentials.
covers provisioning/deprovisioning of internal identities, not the trust model for externally-issued verifiable credentials a verifier accepts.
names trust of suppliers but not the cryptographic trust-anchor model for credentials those entities present.

direct.threat_context: A verifier that accepts verifiable credentials / wallet presentations is a trust boundary: every credential it accepts grants whatever the credential asserts (age, license, employment, entitlement). The high-value abuse is not breaking crypto but exploiting MISSING trust checks — an unpinned issuer key, a skipped revocation check, a replayable presentation, an unverified device binding — to present a forged, revoked, or replayed credential that the verifier treats as authentic. eIDAS 2.0 / EUDI wallets make this a 2026 operational reality across the EU.
direct.framework_lag_declaration: Org identity controls (NIST IA-9/IA-5, ISO A.5.16) assume service-to-service or human-credential auth and are silent on verifiable-credential issuer-trust-anchor pinning, presentation replay-binding, and revocation enforcement. Treat a clean identity-control audit as NON-EVIDENCE for verifier trust posture.

skill_chain.purpose: map the verifier trust model to AAL/IAL/FAL and federation assurance expectations
assess the credential signature-algorithm allowlist for downgrade / non-PQC exposure
separate wallet-certification / signature-valid claims from enforced verifier trust
map findings to the IA-9 / A.5.16 / NIS2 gaps the controls do not cover

artifacts.source: Repository walk for the credential-verifier configuration and call sites: grep for issuerKeyResolver / keyResolver / trustAnchors / trust_anchor / issuer_allowlist / verifyCredential / verifyPresentation across config/*.{js,ts,json,yaml} and the verifier source.
artifacts.description: How the verifier resolves and trusts issuer keys, and whether a trust anchor / issuer allowlist gates acceptance.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: If only source is available (no running config), restrict to the verifier call-sites and their resolver wiring; mark runtime-config-derived trust as inventory_gap.

artifacts.source: grep for status_list / statusList / token-status-list / revocation / verifyStatus across the verifier source and config.
artifacts.description: Whether the verifier resolves and enforces credential revocation / status-list, and how the status-list issuer key is trusted.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect the verifier source for a status-check call on the accept path; if status fetching is runtime-config-gated, mark inventory_gap.

artifacts.source: grep for did:web / did:key / did:jwk / resolveDid / didResolver / .well-known/did.json across source and config.
artifacts.description: Which DID methods are accepted and whether did:web resolution is pinned (cert pin / anchor / known-document-hash).
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Enumerate DID methods referenced in source; absence of pinning logic around did:web fetches is the inspectable signal.

artifacts.source: grep for oid4vp / vp_token / requireKeyBinding / nonce / dcql / presentation_definition / aud across source and config.
artifacts.description: Whether OID4VP presentations require nonce+audience-bound key-binding and whether disclosed claims are filtered to the query.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect the presentation-verify call options for requireKeyBinding + nonce issuance; absence is the signal.

artifacts.source: grep for mdoc / mDL / 18013 / trustAnchorsPem / x5chain / deviceAuth / verifyDeviceAuth across source and config.
artifacts.description: Whether mdoc issuer certificate chains validate against configured trust anchors and whether device-auth is verified.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect mdoc verify call-sites for a device-auth step + trustAnchors argument; omission is the signal.

artifacts.source: grep for openid-federation / trust_anchor / entity_statement / authority_hints / metadata_policy across source and config.
artifacts.description: Whether OpenID Federation trust chains are validated to a pinned anchor and the kid-less / authority-hint policy.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect federation resolution call-sites for an anchor argument and chain-termination check.

artifacts.source: grep for alg / algorithms / allowlist / none / HS256 / verify options across the credential/presentation verify call-sites.
artifacts.description: The signature-algorithm allowlist applied at verification and whether "none"/symmetric algorithms are refused.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect the verify-option objects for an explicit alg allowlist; an implicit/open alg set is the signal.

collection_scope.time_window: current verifier configuration + source state (point-in-time posture audit)
collection_scope.asset_scope: every service that ACCEPTS verifiable credentials or wallet presentations (SD-JWT-VC, OID4VP, mdoc, DID-identified, status-list-governed)

environment_assumptions.assumption: The operator runs at least one credential VERIFIER (accepts credentials), not only an issuer.
environment_assumptions.if_false: If the operator only ISSUES credentials, the verifier indicators do not apply; pivot to issuer-side key-rotation + status-list-publication posture only.

environment_assumptions.assumption: Verifier source or runtime config is readable.
environment_assumptions.if_false: Mark visibility_gap=no_verifier_inventory and report only what the accept-path source reveals.

fallback_if_unavailable.fallback_action: If no DID methods are used (only x5c / federation issuer keys), skip the did:web indicator and note DID-resolution not-in-scope.
If no mdoc/mDL acceptance, skip the device-auth indicator.

indicators.value: A credential verifier resolves the issuer signing key via a callback (issuerKeyResolver / keyResolver / publicKey supplied per-request) with no requirement that the key chain to a pre-configured trust anchor or allowlist.
indicators.description: Without trust-anchor pinning, any party that can present a self-consistent issuer key (e.g. a forged did:web document, an attacker-run OID4VCI issuer) is accepted as a valid issuer — the signature verifies but the issuer identity is unauthenticated.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm whether the verifier passes the resolved key through a trust-anchor / issuer-allowlist check BEFORE accepting the credential — a keyResolver that returns a key for ANY issuer id is the finding; one that returns a key only for allowlisted issuer ids (or validates an x5c/OpenID-Federation chain to an anchor) is safe.

Distinguish a development/test config that intentionally trusts any issuer (often gated behind NODE_ENV !== production) from a production verifier path — only the production-reachable path is the finding.

indicators.value: The verifier accepts a credential without checking the revocation status referenced by its status claim (OAuth Token Status List / status_list index), or fetches the status but ignores a revoked/suspended result.
indicators.description: A revoked or suspended credential continues to validate, so a holder whose authorization was withdrawn (employee offboarded, license suspended) still passes verification.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the verifier actually resolves the status_list endpoint and rejects on a set status bit — a code path that reads the status claim but never compares the bit, or treats a fetch error as "valid", is the finding.

A credential type that genuinely has no revocation model (single-use, short-TTL with no status claim) is not a finding; check the credential schema declares no status mechanism rather than the verifier skipping an existing one.

indicators.value: A credential issuer identified as did:web is resolved by fetching https://<host>/.well-known/did.json with no certificate pinning, CAA check, or federation-anchor validation of <host>.
indicators.description: A network/DNS adversary (or a typosquatted issuer host) can serve a did.json carrying an attacker-controlled key, and the verifier binds the credential to that forged key.
false_positive_checks_required: did:web over a host the operator fully controls AND pins (cert pin / known-good DID-document hash / federation anchor) is safe — the finding is unpinned resolution of an externally-controlled host.
did:key / did:jwk are self-certifying (the key is in the identifier) and are NOT this finding; restrict to did:web (and did:webvh without witness verification).

indicators.value: An OID4VP / SD-JWT-VC presentation is accepted without requiring a Key-Binding JWT bound to the verifier-issued nonce and audience (requireKeyBinding not set / defaults off).
indicators.description: A presentation captured at one verifier can be replayed to another (or replayed later to the same verifier) because nothing ties the presentation to this challenge and this audience.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the verifier issues a fresh nonce per request AND requires the KB-JWT to echo that exact nonce + the verifier audience — a verifier that omits the nonce, accepts any audience, or makes key-binding optional is the finding.
A flow that is genuinely holder-binding-exempt (e.g. an issuer-to-issuer batch import over a mutually-authenticated channel) is out of scope; only holder-presentation verifier endpoints are in scope.

indicators.value: An ISO 18013-5 mdoc presentation has its issuer-signed data verified (MSO digest match) but the device-auth (device signature over the session transcript) is not verified.
indicators.description: A holder can replay issuer-signed mdoc data without proving possession of the device key — the credential is no longer bound to the presenting device.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the verifier calls device-auth verification (device-signature over the session transcript / handover) in addition to issuer-signed verification — verifying only the MSO is the finding.

A trust model that intentionally accepts issuer-signed-only data (e.g. a server-retrieval flow with a separately authenticated channel) is out of scope; the finding is a device-retrieval flow skipping device auth.

indicators.value: The verifier does not enforce a signature-algorithm allowlist at credential / presentation verification, or the allowlist permits weak or attacker-selectable algorithms (HMAC alongside asymmetric, "none", non-PQC where a PQC posture is required).
indicators.description: An attacker can forge a credential using an algorithm the library happens to support but the issuer never uses (algorithm-substitution), or a downgrade to a weak primitive.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm an explicit alg allowlist is enforced AND "none" is always refused AND symmetric (HMAC) algs are not accepted on a path expecting asymmetric issuer signatures — absence of any of these is the finding.
A single-algorithm deployment that hard-codes one asymmetric alg (effectively an allowlist of one) is safe; the finding is an open or implicit alg set.

indicators.value: A holder-supplied key attestation (claiming the credential private key is hardware/TEE-backed) is accepted without a verifier callback that validates the attestation chain (requireKeyAttestation off / no keyAttestationVerifier).
indicators.description: A holder can claim hardware key protection it does not have, defeating a policy that grants higher assurance to attested keys.
false_positive_checks_required: Only a finding where policy actually RELIES on the attestation (e.g. grants AAL3-equivalent or higher entitlements to attested keys) — if attestation is informational and unused, downgrade.
Confirm the verifier validates the attestation signature chain to a known attestation root (device-vendor CA) rather than accepting the attestation JWT on its face.

indicators.value: OpenID Federation trust resolution applies metadata policy / accepts entity statements without validating the chain terminates at a pinned trust anchor, or accepts kid-less JWKS / unbounded authority_hints traversal.
indicators.description: A leaf or intermediate not actually subordinate to a configured anchor is treated as trusted, so federation membership (and the metadata it grants) is forgeable.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the resolved trust chain is validated to terminate at an operator-configured trust anchor (not merely "a self-consistent chain"), and that kid-less single-key JWKS acceptance is opt-in not default.
A single-anchor deployment with the anchor pinned is safe; the finding is anchor-less or any-anchor chain acceptance.

indicators.value: An OID4VP verifier accepts a presentation that discloses claims beyond the DCQL / presentation-definition query, because claim filtering is applied after (not as part of) acceptance.
indicators.description: The verifier ingests and may log/store personal claims it did not request (e.g. full DOB, address, government ID number when only age-over-18 was asked) — a data-minimisation and privacy-by-design failure.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the verifier rejects or strips claims outside the requested set rather than accepting and processing the full disclosed claim set — accepting over-disclosed claims into the verified object is the finding.
A verifier that requests a broad claim set deliberately (and is authorised to) is not over-disclosure; compare the DCQL/presentation-definition to what is accepted.

indicators.value: The status-list JWT is verified with a key resolved for the status-list endpoint domain without confirming that endpoint is within the credential issuer's declared trust scope.
indicators.description: A credential issuer (or an attacker who can influence the status_list URI) can point revocation at an endpoint they control and always answer "valid", neutralising revocation.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the status-list issuer key is validated against the SAME trust anchor / issuer identity as the credential it governs, not merely against whatever key the status-list endpoint presents.
A deployment where issuer and status-list authority are intentionally the same pinned key is safe; the finding is an unpinned or cross-issuer status-list trust.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: A test harness that injects a per-test issuer key via keyResolver.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: The keyResolver is reachable only when NODE_ENV/test flags are set; the production verifier path validates against a pinned issuer allowlist or federation anchor.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Short-TTL single-use credentials with no status claim at all.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: The credential schema declares no status mechanism — there is nothing to check, versus a status claim present but unread.
The identifier embeds the key (did:key/did:jwk) so no network fetch occurs; only did:web fetches an external document.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Issuer-to-issuer batch import over a mutually authenticated channel.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: The endpoint is a holder-presentation verifier (accepts wallet vp_token) rather than a server-to-server channel with its own auth.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Server-retrieval flow with a separately authenticated transport.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: A device-retrieval (proximity/QR) flow that omits device-auth is the finding; server-retrieval with channel auth is not.
One fixed asymmetric alg is an allowlist of one; an open/implicit alg set or accepted HMAC/"none" is the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Attestation present but informational and unused by policy.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Policy grants higher entitlements to attested keys (finding) versus attestation logged but not relied upon (downgrade).

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Single-anchor deployment with the anchor pinned.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: The chain is validated to terminate at a configured anchor (safe) versus any self-consistent chain accepted (finding).

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: A verifier authorised to request a broad claim set.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Compare the DCQL/presentation-definition to the accepted claims — accepting claims beyond the query is the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Issuer and status-list authority are the same pinned key.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Status-list key validated against the credential issuer anchor (safe) versus against whatever the status endpoint presents (finding).

minimum_signal.detected: At least one verifier accept-path lacks a trust check that policy relies on (issuer not anchored, revocation not enforced, presentation replayable, device-auth skipped, or open algorithm set) on a production-reachable verifier.
minimum_signal.inconclusive: A trust check is ambiguous (e.g. anchoring done in a runtime config the audit could not read) — record as visibility_gap, not a clean result.
minimum_signal.not_detected: Every accepted credential format validates the issuer key against a pinned anchor, enforces revocation, binds presentations to a fresh nonce+audience, verifies device-auth where applicable, and enforces an explicit algorithm allowlist.

blast_radius_model.scope_question: How many credential types and downstream entitlements does the un-trusted verifier path gate, and is it internet-reachable?

scoring_rubric.condition: Verifier is internet-facing and the un-anchored path gates a high-value entitlement (payment, age-gated access, employment/licence assertion)
scoring_rubric.description: Forged/replayed credential yields direct authorization to a high-value action

scoring_rubric.condition: Verifier accepts credentials but the entitlement is low-value or human-reviewed downstream
scoring_rubric.description: Forged credential needs a second factor / human step to cause impact

scoring_rubric.condition: Verifier is internal-only / behind strong network controls

compliance_theater_check.claim: We accept a certified wallet / the credential signature verifies, so credential acceptance is trustworthy.
compliance_theater_check.audit_evidence: Wallet certification documents, a verify() call that returns success, a credential schema that declares a status_list.
compliance_theater_check.reality_test: Show the verifier trust-anchor / issuer-allowlist config, the revocation-check on the accept path, and the presentation nonce+audience binding. If acceptance succeeds against an unpinned issuer key, a never-read status list, or an unbound presentation, the assurance is paper.

framework_gap_mapping.actual_gap: IA-9 does not require credential issuer trust-anchor pinning; a verified signature from an unanchored issuer passes IA-9 as written.
A.5.16 governs internal identity deprovisioning, not enforcement of externally-issued credential revocation at the verifier.

remediation_paths.description: Replace open issuerKeyResolver acceptance with an issuer allowlist / x5c-or-federation chain validated to a configured trust anchor; reject issuer keys that do not chain to it.
Resolve the status_list on the accept path and reject on a set status bit; treat a status fetch failure as fail-closed (not "valid").
Issue a fresh per-request nonce, require a Key-Binding JWT echoing that nonce + the verifier audience, and verify device-auth for mdoc; reject unbound presentations.
Enforce an explicit signature-algorithm allowlist (asymmetric only on issuer paths; "none" always refused; PQC-ready per the org crypto posture) at every verify call.
Strip or reject claims disclosed beyond the DCQL / presentation-definition query before processing, and validate key attestation only against a known attestation root.

validation_tests.test: Present a credential signed by a well-formed but non-allowlisted issuer key (e.g. a fresh did:web the verifier was not configured to trust).
validation_tests.expected_result: Verifier rejects: issuer key does not chain to a configured trust anchor.

validation_tests.test: Present a structurally valid credential whose status-list bit is set to revoked.
validation_tests.expected_result: Verifier rejects on revoked status; a status-fetch failure also rejects (fail-closed).

validation_tests.test: Capture a valid presentation and replay it with a stale nonce / to a different audience.
validation_tests.expected_result: Verifier rejects: KB-JWT nonce/audience does not match the issued challenge.

validation_tests.test: Present a valid, unrevoked, anchored credential with a correctly bound presentation.
validation_tests.expected_result: Verifier accepts — no regression on the legitimate path.

residual_risk_statement.risk: A trusted issuer whose own signing key is compromised can still mint credentials the verifier will accept until the issuer is removed from the anchor set.
residual_risk_statement.why_remains: Trust-anchor pinning authenticates the issuer identity, not the issuer's internal key hygiene; cross-issuer compromise is out of the verifier's control.

evidence_requirements.description: The issuer allowlist / trust-anchor set, revocation-check wiring, presentation-binding options, and algorithm allowlist as configured at audit time.
Outcomes of the forged-issuer / revoked / replayed negative tests plus the legitimate-path functional test.

regression_trigger.condition: A new credential format / DID method / federation is accepted
Periodic re-attestation of verifier trust posture

lesson_template.attack_vector: Forged / revoked / replayed verifiable credential accepted by a verifier missing a trust check (unanchored issuer, unenforced revocation, unbound presentation, skipped device-auth, open algorithm set).
lesson_template.control_gap: Verifier accept-path lacks issuer trust-anchor pinning / revocation enforcement / presentation replay-binding.
lesson_template.framework_gap: NIST IA-9 + ISO A.5.16 + NIS2 Art.21 do not prescribe verifiable-credential verifier trust-anchor / revocation / replay controls.
lesson_template.new_control_requirement: Verifier MUST validate issuer keys to a pinned anchor, enforce revocation fail-closed, and bind presentations to a fresh nonce + audience.

exception_generation.trigger_condition: A verifier path cannot pin a trust anchor in time (e.g. an open ecosystem with no central anchor yet).

exception_template.scope: the specific verifier endpoint + credential type
compensating_controls: downstream human review of high-value entitlements
enhanced logging of accepted issuer identities

regression_schedule.next_run: quarterly or on any new accepted credential format
regression_schedule.trigger: change to accepted issuers / DID methods / federations, or quarterly re-attestation

directives.title: Inventory + trust-test every verifiable-credential / wallet acceptance path
Targeted sweep for EUDI / mDL wallet acceptance trust posture
